Links: [{"title":"macOS Desktop Audio Capture Guide | OBS","url":"https://obsproject.com/kb/macos-desktop-audio-capture-guide"},{"title":"Mac Audio Routing Virtual Cable EZ solution | OBS Forums","url":"https://obsproject.com/forum/threads/mac-audio-routing-virtual-cable-ez-solution.124415/"},{"title":"virtual audio cable | OBS Forums","url":"https://obsproject.com/forum/tags/virtual-audio-cable/"},{"title":"How to capture desktop audio on Mac? | OBS Forums","url":"https://obsproject.com/forum/threads/how-to-capture-desktop-audio-on-mac.148146/"},{"title":"Virtual Audio Cable for Zoom | OBS Forums","url":"https://obsproject.com/forum/threads/virtual-audio-cable-for-zoom.125072/"},{"title":"How To Record Desktop Audio on Mac With OBS Studio","url":"https://democreator.wondershare.com/audio-recorder/obs-desktop-audio-mac.html"},{"title":"Using Virtual Audio Cables in OBS with Zoom - StreamGeeks","url":"https://streamgeeks.us/using-virtual-audio-cables-in-obs-with-zoom/"},{"title":"VB-Audio Virtual Apps","url":"https://vb-audio.com/Cable/"},{"title":"Configuring your Audio Interface in OBS on macOS вҖ“ Focusrite","url":"https://support.focusrite.com/hc/en-gb/articles/360012954760-Configuring-your-Audio-Interface-in-OBS-on-macOS"},{"title":"OBS Studio - Advanced Audio Capture - Mac OS X | OBS Forums","url":"https://obsproject.com/forum/threads/advanced-audio-capture-mac-os-x.22420/"}]

Based on the search results, here's comprehensive information about recording system audio in OBS Studio on macOS using virtual audio cable solutions in 2024:

## Native macOS Audio Capture (2024)

When using OBS Studio 28 and 29 on macOS 13 (Ventura) and higher, you can capture desktop audio or just the audio coming from a single application.(no content)If you have Ventura (macOS 13) or newer, you can use OBS Studio 30 to capture desktop or application audio.

## Virtual Audio Cable Solutions

For older macOS versions or when you need more control, virtual audio cables are necessary. In order to capture desktop audio on older versions of macOS, you will require additional software.

### VB-Cable (Popular in 2024)

VB-CABLE by VB-Audio can be used to record macOS desktop audio.(no content)VB-CABLE is a virtual audio device working as virtual audio cable. 

Setup process for VB-Cable:
1. Set VB-Cable as an Output source in the computer's settings. Set VB-Cable as a Device in Desctop Audio of OBS. Go to Advance Audio Properties of Desktop Audio of OBS and set the Audio Monitoring to Monitor and Output.(no content)You can download two virtual audio cables for free from VB-Audio.com for your Mac or Windows computer. The VB-CABLE drivers are donationware, so they will ask you to make an optional donation to the developers.

### BlackHole (Free Alternative)

A much better and free option is to use Blackhole, which is a successor to Soundflower. It is also completely free and works on the latest Apple M1 silicon.(no content)Blackhole works and free: https://existential.audio/blackhole/ there are youtube contents to show how to install and run it.

## Limitations and Considerations

If, however, you have an older macOS version and an older OBS version, things are a bit more complicated. OBS 28 and 29, for instance, require you to add your screen capture source to capture app audio on Ventura. If you have an older macOS version, on the other hand, no version of OBS will be able to capture desktop audio by itself.(no content)Setting up virtual audio cables to work with Zoom and OBS is more complicated than using the built-in virtual camera feature. OBS does not offer built-in virtual audio cable support at this time and therefore, you will need to download virtual audio cables to send audio from OBS to other applications on your computer.

## Key Takeaways for 2024

1. **macOS Ventura (13) or newer**: Can use OBS Studio 28+ directly for desktop audio capture
2. **Older macOS versions**: Require virtual audio cable software like VB-Cable or BlackHole
3. **VB-Cable**: Free (donationware) solution that works well with current macOS versions
4. **BlackHole**: Completely free, open-source alternative that works on Apple Silicon Macs
5. **Setup complexity**: Virtual audio cables require additional configuration but provide more routing flexibility

The virtual audio cable solutions remain essential for advanced audio routing scenarios, even in 2024, especially when working with multiple applications or older macOS versions.

[Atlassian cookies and tracking notice, (opens new window)](https://www.atlassian.com/legal/cookies) PreferencesOnly necessaryAccept all Moody College of Communication / How to set up BlackHole Audio on a Mac [restrictions.empty](https://cloud.wikis.utexas.edu/wiki/spaces/comm/pages/33425619/How+to+set+up+BlackHole+Audio+on+a+Mac) [Jira links](https://cloud.wikis.utexas.edu/wiki/spaces/comm/pages/33425619/How+to+set+up+BlackHole+Audio+on+a+Mac) - ![]() More actions Search # How to set up BlackHole Audio on a Mac - ![](https://cloud.wikis.utexas.edu/wiki/aa-avatar/5eebce97a6a7110ab574081d) Owned by [Mark Rogers](https://home.atlassian.com/o/41e4dab1-244a-4009-afc6-de20b9727e96/people/5eebce97a6a7110ab574081d?cloudId=b29d0c3f-8904-4032-a6a4-063cb1682555) Last updated: [Jun 17, 2020](https://cloud.wikis.utexas.edu/wiki/pages/diffpagesbyversion.action?pageId=33425619&selectedPageVersions=5&selectedPageVersions=6) [Legacy editor](https://cloud.wikis.utexas.edu/wiki/spaces/comm/pages/33425619/www.atlassian.com) ## BlackHole is a modern macOS virtual audio driver that allows applications to pass audio to other applications with zero additional latency. You can download the latest installer for Mac [here](https://existential.audio/blackhole/?pk_campaign=github&pk_kwd=release). В  You will need to enter your email address to be sent the download link. This version of BlackHole is compatible with macOS Catalina. В  В You will also need to allow the app to be installed in the Security and Privacy setting in System Preferences. Once it is installed follow the steps below to set up the correct Input and Output for your computer. | | | | --- | --- | | First you will need to create a new INPUT and OUTPUT
Open the Audio Midi Setup: _Applications/Utilities/Audio Midi Setup_ | ![](https://cloud.wikis.utexas.edu/wiki/download/thumbnails/33425619/midi-controller-audio-midi-setup-icon-png-favpng-DEaRVH1nTZcNqU57mpS98P342%20copy.jpg?version=1&modificationDate=1592316694000&cacheVersion=1&api=v2&width=152&height=150) | | ### This will create the custom OUTPUT
Click on the **PLUS sign** and then choose **Create Multi-Output Device** | ![](https://cloud.wikis.utexas.edu/wiki/download/thumbnails/33425619/1-create%20multi-output.jpg?version=1&modificationDate=1592316694000&cacheVersion=1&api=v2&width=696&height=400) | | **Check the box for Built-In Output**
**Then check the box for BlackHole 16ch**
_note: it is important that you first check the box for Built-in Output_
_before you check the box for BlackHole 16ch. Built-in Output should_
_be first in the list_
**Then check the box for Drift Correction for BlackHole 16ch**
Double check your settings and make sure they look exactly like you
see them in the screenshot. | ![](https://cloud.wikis.utexas.edu/wiki/download/thumbnails/33425619/2-OUTPUT-Built-in-BlackHole.jpg?version=1&modificationDate=1592316751000&cacheVersion=1&api=v2&width=772&height=400) | | ### This will create the custom INPUT
Click on the **PLUS sign** and then choose **Create Aggregate Device** | ![](https://cloud.wikis.utexas.edu/wiki/download/thumbnails/33425619/3-Create%20INPUT.jpg?version=1&modificationDate=1592316694000&cacheVersion=1&api=v2&width=724&height=400) | | **Check the box for BlackHole 16ch**
**Then check the box for your Microphone**
_In this example the microphone is a Rode VideoMic NTG._
_If you use an external microphone choose it. If you use a_
_laptop the microphone will be the Built-in Microphone. If you_
_use an external webcam the microphone on the webcam will_
_also be an option listed._
**Check the box for Drift Correction for the microphone**
Double check your settings and make sure they look exactly like you
see them in the screenshot | ![](https://cloud.wikis.utexas.edu/wiki/download/thumbnails/33425619/4-BlackHole-Rode%20Mic%20INPUT.jpg?version=3&modificationDate=1592340026000&cacheVersion=1&api=v2&width=772&height=400) | | If you want, you can rename the default names (Multi-Output Device & Aggregate Device)
by clicking on the names.
_In this example they were renamed as Built-in Output + BlackHole and BlackHole Input_ | ![](https://cloud.wikis.utexas.edu/wiki/download/thumbnails/33425619/5-Rename.jpg?version=1&modificationDate=1592340176000&cacheVersion=1&api=v2&width=772&height=400) | | And finally the last step is to choose this new INPUT and OUTPUT in the SOUND settings under SYSTEM PREFERENCES.
Under the OUTPUT tab вҶ’ choose the newly created output
(in this example it is Built-In + BlackHole)
Under the INPUT tab вҶ’ choose the newly created input
(in this example it is BlackHole Input) | ![](https://cloud.wikis.utexas.edu/wiki/download/thumbnails/33425619/Output%20-%20settings.png?version=2&modificationDate=1592316694000&cacheVersion=1&api=v2&width=335&height=250)![](https://cloud.wikis.utexas.edu/wiki/download/thumbnails/33425619/INPUT%20-%20controls.png?version=2&modificationDate=1592316694000&cacheVersion=1&api=v2&width=334&height=250) | ![thumbs up](https://pf-emoji-service--cdn.us-east-1.prod.public.atl-paas.net/standard/ef8b0642-7523-4e13-9fd3-01b65648acf6/32x32/1f44d.png) ![clapping hands](https://pf-emoji-service--cdn.us-east-1.prod.public.atl-paas.net/standard/ef8b0642-7523-4e13-9fd3-01b65648acf6/32x32/1f44f.png) ![party popper](https://pf-emoji-service--cdn.us-east-1.prod.public.atl-paas.net/standard/ef8b0642-7523-4e13-9fd3-01b65648acf6/32x32/1f389.png) [Confluence Documentation](https://support.atlassian.com/confluence-cloud/resources/) \| [Web Privacy Policy](https://www.utexas.edu/web-privacy-policy) \| [Web Accessibility](https://www.utexas.edu/web-accessibility-policy "Web Accessibility") {"serverDuration": 31, "requestCorrelationId": "4e5edb76b85c4f4483584c848f7bc40f"}

input.query: Whisper AI Obsidian plugin audio transcription meeting notes

content.text:
URL: https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480 Title: Speech-to-text in Obsidian using OpenAI Whisper Service Description: An AI speech-to-text service that is rather amazing. It understands multiple languages, cost-effective & fast. Content: [Skip to main content](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480#main-container) # [Speech-to-text in Obsidian using OpenAI Whisper Service](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480) [Share & showcase](https://forum.obsidian.md/c/share-showcase/9) You have selected **0** posts. [select all](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480) [cancel selecting](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480) 12.8k views 8 likes 7 links [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/user_avatar/forum.obsidian.md/typopsycho/48/29441_2.png)3](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TypoPsycho "TypoPsycho") [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/user_avatar/forum.obsidian.md/tfthacker/48/12958_2.png)3](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TfTHacker "TfTHacker") [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/letter_avatar_proxy/v4/letter/s/ebca7d/48.png)2](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/SudoRmRfMe "SudoRmRfMe") [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/letter_avatar_proxy/v4/letter/l/3d9bf3/48.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/lymeswold "lymeswold") [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/letter_avatar_proxy/v4/letter/a/ad7895/48.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/aipower "aipower") [Apr 2023](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480/1 "Jump to the first post") 2 / 10 Jun 2023 [Mar 2024](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480/10) [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/user_avatar/forum.obsidian.md/tfthacker/48/12958_2.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TfTHacker) [TfTHacker](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TfTHacker)VIP [Apr 2023](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480 "Post date") The same people who brought us chatGPT have also made an AI speech-to-text service that is rather amazing. It understands multiple languages, cost-effective & fast. In this article, I profile an Obsidian plugin by [https://twitter.com/nikdanilov](https://twitter.com/nikdanilov) using the Whisper Service - [Using Obsidian as audio vault?](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/using-obsidian-as-audio-vault/82272/2) 12.8k views 8 likes 7 links [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/user_avatar/forum.obsidian.md/typopsycho/48/29441_2.png)3](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TypoPsycho "TypoPsycho") [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/user_avatar/forum.obsidian.md/tfthacker/48/12958_2.png)3](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TfTHacker "TfTHacker") [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/letter_avatar_proxy/v4/letter/s/ebca7d/48.png)2](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/SudoRmRfMe "SudoRmRfMe") [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/letter_avatar_proxy/v4/letter/l/3d9bf3/48.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/lymeswold "lymeswold") [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/letter_avatar_proxy/v4/letter/a/ad7895/48.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/aipower "aipower") 1 month later [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/user_avatar/forum.obsidian.md/typopsycho/48/29441_2.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TypoPsycho) [TypoPsycho](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TypoPsycho) [Jun 2023](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480/2 "Post date") This is a really fantastic plugin! Thanks for this. Would you have any idea how I can transcribe longer recordings I already made in .mp4 format? [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/user_avatar/forum.obsidian.md/tfthacker/48/12958_2.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TfTHacker) [TfTHacker](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TfTHacker)VIP [Jun 2023](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480/3 "Post date") Good question. I would suggest posting it as a feature request at the developerвҖҷs GitHub repo for this plugin: [Issues В· nikdanilov/whisper-obsidian-plugin В· GitHub](https://github.com/nikdanilov/whisper-obsidian-plugin/issues) Another option is this plugin, which seems to do what you want but I am unsure if there are additional charges. [GitHub - djmango/obsidian-transcription: Obsidian plugin to create high-quality transcriptions from markdown linked audio files](https://github.com/djmango/obsidian-transcription). I hope you find a good solution, I see a few have been interested in the same thing. [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/user_avatar/forum.obsidian.md/typopsycho/48/29441_2.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TypoPsycho) [TypoPsycho](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TypoPsycho) [Jun 2023](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480/4 "Post date") Many thanks! I will post the feature request. regarding the /obsidian-transcription: This requires signing up to [https://scribe.gambitengine.com](https://scribe.gambitengine.com/) which I do not exactly understand why this is required as direct use of OpenAI API is possible to access Whisper (I just donвҖҷt know how). [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/user_avatar/forum.obsidian.md/tfthacker/48/12958_2.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TfTHacker) [TfTHacker](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TfTHacker)VIP [Jun 2023](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480/5 "Post date") You donвҖҷt know how to do what? [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/user_avatar/forum.obsidian.md/typopsycho/48/29441_2.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TypoPsycho) [TypoPsycho](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TypoPsycho) [Jun 2023](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480/6 "Post date") Many thanks for your question. I did not know how to upload files to Whisper directly using my personal API. In the meantime, I have found it amazingly easy to install Whisper locally on my Mac and to run transcriptions in Terminal via this instruction. [Install Whisper.cpp on your Mac in 5mn and transcribe all your podcasts for free!](https://blog.castopod.org/install-whisper-cpp-on-your-mac-in-5mn-and-transcribe-all-your-podcasts-for-free/). I would love to see this as an Obsidian plugin, but in the mean time have found a good alternative solution. 2 months later [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/letter_avatar_proxy/v4/letter/a/ad7895/48.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/aipower) [aipower](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/aipower) [Aug 2023](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480/7 "Post date") I used Obsidian вҖңWhisper: Upload audio fileвҖқ; Whisper store the audio data? 4 months later [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/letter_avatar_proxy/v4/letter/l/3d9bf3/48.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/lymeswold) [lymeswold](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/lymeswold) [Dec 2023](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480/8 "Post date") IвҖҷve just installed the Whisper plugin, created an API key and configured the plugin. No success yet as IвҖҷm seeing error 429, presumably returned by Whisper вҖҰ вҖңRate limit reached for requestsвҖқ. I think I read that I get $18 free credit to being with, but IвҖҷm unclear whether I need to set up a billing method first, which could be the cause of this error. Any help appreciated! 2 months later [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/letter_avatar_proxy/v4/letter/s/ebca7d/48.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/SudoRmRfMe) [SudoRmRfMe](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/SudoRmRfMe)VIP [Mar 2024](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480/9 "Post date") Genius actually, as I was working on this myself. probably end up using this compared to my Franken Python of a mess. IвҖҷll have to see maybe if I could incorporate my real time dictation into something like this, IвҖҷll post it if I end up having something work. [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/letter_avatar_proxy/v4/letter/s/ebca7d/48.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/SudoRmRfMe) [SudoRmRfMe](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/SudoRmRfMe)VIP ![](https://forum.obsidian.md/letter_avatar_proxy/v4/letter/l/3d9bf3/24.png)lymeswold [Mar 2024](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480/10 "Post date") Do you have GPT plus? IвҖҷll look into this actually, as when I was using it, the only credit you were using was killing your GPU. IвҖҷll update if I find a work around, I just came across this, so hopefully IвҖҷll find one. -Alana Reply ### New & Unread Topics | Topic | Replies | Views | Activity | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | [Apply obsidian tags to MacOS file system \[Hazel script\]](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/apply-obsidian-tags-to-macos-file-system-hazel-script/100874)
[Share & showcase](https://forum.obsidian.md/c/share-showcase/9) | [0](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/apply-obsidian-tags-to-macos-file-system-hazel-script/100874/1) | 43 | [May 19](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/apply-obsidian-tags-to-macos-file-system-hazel-script/100874/1) | | [3D Embed Plugin Showcase](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/3d-embed-plugin-showcase/92854)
[Share & showcase](https://forum.obsidian.md/c/share-showcase/9)

Welcome to The Obsidian Observer, a hub for all Obsidian enthusiasts. Whether youвҖҷre a beginner or a seasoned pro, our publication delivers in-depth how-to guides, innovative workflows, and captivating opinions to help unlock your note-taking potential. [Follow publication](https://medium.com/m/signin?actionUrl=https%3A%2F%2Fmedium.com%2F_%2Fsubscribe%2Fcollection%2Fobsidian-observer&operation=register&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fmedium.com%2Fobsidian-observer%2Fspeech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service-7b2843bf8d64&collection=Obsidian+Observer&collectionId=dc0a52745260&source=post_page---post_publication_sidebar-dc0a52745260-7b2843bf8d64---------------------post_publication_sidebar------------------) # Speech-to-text in Obsidian using OpenAI Whisper Service [![TfTHacker](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fill:32:32/1*ZV47V0sTbc72zve9dqnllw.jpeg)](https://medium.com/@tfthacker?source=post_page---byline--7b2843bf8d64---------------------------------------) [TfTHacker](https://medium.com/@tfthacker?source=post_page---byline--7b2843bf8d64---------------------------------------) Follow 5 min read В· Apr 22, 2023 118 2 [Listen](https://medium.com/m/signin?actionUrl=https%3A%2F%2Fmedium.com%2Fplans%3Fdimension%3Dpost_audio_button%26postId%3D7b2843bf8d64&operation=register&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fmedium.com%2Fobsidian-observer%2Fspeech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service-7b2843bf8d64&source=---header_actions--7b2843bf8d64---------------------post_audio_button------------------) Share The Obsidian community continues to impress. This month there are several new and updated AI plugins. In this article, I want to highlight one of them that stood out to me that has a lot of potential: The [Whisper plugin](https://obsidian.md/plugins?id=whisper) by [Nik Danilov](https://twitter.com/nikdanilov_). Press enter or click to view image in full size ![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:700/1*cGhCPzrZZH_bEBMiblC8GA.jpeg) > **Please note**, [full disclosure](https://medium.com/@tfthacker/writers-please-write-from-the-heart-not-from-ai-684960e0b44c), I used AI tools to assist in the writing of this article. (Grammarly, gpt-4, and Whisper). # What is Whisper? Whisper is a service provided by [OpenAI](https://openai.com/). Whisper is an Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) system, which means it can convert spoken language into written text. In simpler terms, itвҖҷs a technology that вҖңlistensвҖқ to human speech and transcribes it into text. Whisper is designed to understand and process a wide variety of languages and accents, making it useful for numerous applications. Some common use cases for ASR systems like Whisper include transcription services, voice assistants, real-time captions, and more. To develop Whisper, OpenAI trained the system on a vast amount of multilingual and multitask data collected from the web. This extensive training allows the system to provide accurate transcriptions for everyday computer users, even if they have different accents or speak various languages. # Why do I like Whisper? **First**, it does a _good job and handles multiple languages_. I frequently work in two languages and the ability to do speech-to-text without having to think about it is wonderful. **Second**, it is a _cost-effective_ speech-to-text service. You can request an API key from openAI and you control how much you spend. The API cost is $0.006 a minute. So ten minutes of dictation is 6 pennies. # Using Whisper in Obsidian ## Installation The Whisper plugin can be installed via the Community Plugins tab in Settings in Obsidian. ## API Key Once installed you need to provide it with some information for it to work. As mentioned, Whisper is built on top of the OpenAI Whisper service. To use it, you need an OpenAI API key. If you donвҖҷt have one, you can read this article to get instructions on how to acquire your own key: вҖң [_How to get your own API Key for using OpenAI chatGPT in Obsidian_](https://tfthacker.medium.com/how-to-get-your-own-api-key-for-using-openai-chatgpt-in-obsidian-41b7dd71f8d3)вҖқ Once you obtain your API key from OpenAI, paste it into the API Key field in the Settings tab for the Whisper plugin as illustrated in the following image: Press enter or click to view image in full size ![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:700/1*NWzTOaTdglNrOtF6qapKJA.png) Provide the Whisper plugin with your OpenAI API Key ## Folder for Output by the Plugin The second thing to configure is the template file location. It is called template location, which is a little confusing since it's not a template folder. This is the folder where speech-to-text documents will be stored. I left the folder blank which will put any documents created by the plugin into the root folder of the Obsidian vault. # Using the Plugin The plugin is very simple in what it does. You dictate to it and it creates a markdown file with a transcription. Take a look at the following animated image: Press enter or click to view image in full size ![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:700/1*rmkBXt2TkWu42wPG2zpG4A.gif) There is no audio recorded here, but I was using the plugin. - First, you click on the button on the ribbon bar. It is a little waveform-looking button. - Then you are prompted with a modal for recording. - Click on record and start talking. - When you finish, click the stop button At this point, the plugin will send your audio recording to the Whisper API. - Then the Whisper API will convert your speech into text and return it to the plugin. - The plugin then creates a new markdown file and inserts the transcribed speech. Since this is now saved to a markdown file, you can do all your normal editing in Obsidian as you would do with any other file. # Add this Plugin to our Workflow I will use this plugin in two ways: ## Rapid Capturing of Thought This plugin should encourage us to use speech-to-text to rapidly capture our thoughts. Sometimes I am just too lazy to type, so clicking a button and capturing a thought while it's fresh on the mind and not having to fiddle with the keyboard or editor is a way of reducing mental friction and not losing the idea. ## Long-form Thinking Out loud While the goal of note-taking is to capture our thoughts, there are some subtle differences in how we write when using a keyboard and how we think when expressing ourselves verbally. Each form of capturing thoughts has its advantages. I find speaking very freeing, but also sloppy, allowing for more random thoughts to emerge along with lots of mistakes. It is a bit like speaking with yourself out loud and it gets messy. But at the same time, hearing your thoughts audibly triggers our minds in unique ways. So while taking notes, sometimes I just hit the record button and start talking to Obsidian and allowing the ideas to flow, not being bogged down by the rigor of typing in words, sentences, punctuation, and all the rest of the rigor of writing. Put simply, speaking without restraint can be creative and still capture those words into text that can then be edited later. # The Future of This Plugin This plugin was just released and it needs polishing. Here is what I would like to see: - Ability to transcribe into an existing document at the current cursor location. - Moving the Recording modal into a side pane. In the pane have the recording controls, while still allowing us to freely move around the Obsidian UI as we continue to dictate our thoughts. Our words could even be transcribed into the pane with the ability to easily copy them into my notes later. The idea is to let me reference other notes and Obsidian features while dictating my ideas Having said all this, this plugin is off to a promising start and I look forward to seeing what its developer does with it. Even more so, I am happy to have it as a part of my Obsidian workflow. Thank you for reading this article. It understands multiple languages, cost-effective & fast. In this article, I profile an Obsidian plugin by [https://twitter.com/nikdanilov](https://twitter.com/nikdanilov) using the Whisper Service - [Using Obsidian as audio vault?](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/using-obsidian-as-audio-vault/82272/2) 12.8k views 8 likes 7 links [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/user_avatar/forum.obsidian.md/typopsycho/48/29441_2.png)3](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TypoPsycho "TypoPsycho") [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/user_avatar/forum.obsidian.md/tfthacker/48/12958_2.png)3](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TfTHacker "TfTHacker") [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/letter_avatar_proxy/v4/letter/s/ebca7d/48.png)2](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/SudoRmRfMe "SudoRmRfMe") [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/letter_avatar_proxy/v4/letter/l/3d9bf3/48.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/lymeswold "lymeswold") [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/letter_avatar_proxy/v4/letter/a/ad7895/48.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/aipower "aipower") 1 month later [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/user_avatar/forum.obsidian.md/typopsycho/48/29441_2.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TypoPsycho) [TypoPsycho](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TypoPsycho) [Jun 2023](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480/2 "Post date") This is a really fantastic plugin! Thanks for this. Would you have any idea how I can transcribe longer recordings I already made in .mp4 format? [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/user_avatar/forum.obsidian.md/tfthacker/48/12958_2.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TfTHacker) [TfTHacker](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TfTHacker)VIP [Jun 2023](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480/3 "Post date") Good question. I would suggest posting it as a feature request at the developerвҖҷs GitHub repo for this plugin: [Issues В· nikdanilov/whisper-obsidian-plugin В· GitHub](https://github.com/nikdanilov/whisper-obsidian-plugin/issues) Another option is this plugin, which seems to do what you want but I am unsure if there are additional charges. [GitHub - djmango/obsidian-transcription: Obsidian plugin to create high-quality transcriptions from markdown linked audio files](https://github.com/djmango/obsidian-transcription). I hope you find a good solution, I see a few have been interested in the same thing. [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/user_avatar/forum.obsidian.md/typopsycho/48/29441_2.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TypoPsycho) [TypoPsycho](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TypoPsycho) [Jun 2023](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480/4 "Post date") Many thanks! I will post the feature request. regarding the /obsidian-transcription: This requires signing up to [https://scribe.gambitengine.com](https://scribe.gambitengine.com/) which I do not exactly understand why this is required as direct use of OpenAI API is possible to access Whisper (I just donвҖҷt know how). [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/user_avatar/forum.obsidian.md/tfthacker/48/12958_2.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TfTHacker) [TfTHacker](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TfTHacker)VIP [Jun 2023](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480/5 "Post date") You donвҖҷt know how to do what? [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/user_avatar/forum.obsidian.md/typopsycho/48/29441_2.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TypoPsycho) [TypoPsycho](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/TypoPsycho) [Jun 2023](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480/6 "Post date") Many thanks for your question. I did not know how to upload files to Whisper directly using my personal API. In the meantime, I have found it amazingly easy to install Whisper locally on my Mac and to run transcriptions in Terminal via this instruction. [Install Whisper.cpp on your Mac in 5mn and transcribe all your podcasts for free!](https://blog.castopod.org/install-whisper-cpp-on-your-mac-in-5mn-and-transcribe-all-your-podcasts-for-free/). I would love to see this as an Obsidian plugin, but in the mean time have found a good alternative solution. 2 months later [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/letter_avatar_proxy/v4/letter/a/ad7895/48.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/aipower) [aipower](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/aipower) [Aug 2023](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480/7 "Post date") I used Obsidian вҖңWhisper: Upload audio fileвҖқ; Whisper store the audio data? 4 months later [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/letter_avatar_proxy/v4/letter/l/3d9bf3/48.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/lymeswold) [lymeswold](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/lymeswold) [Dec 2023](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480/8 "Post date") IвҖҷve just installed the Whisper plugin, created an API key and configured the plugin. No success yet as IвҖҷm seeing error 429, presumably returned by Whisper вҖҰ вҖңRate limit reached for requestsвҖқ. I think I read that I get $18 free credit to being with, but IвҖҷm unclear whether I need to set up a billing method first, which could be the cause of this error. Any help appreciated! 2 months later [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/letter_avatar_proxy/v4/letter/s/ebca7d/48.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/SudoRmRfMe) [SudoRmRfMe](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/SudoRmRfMe)VIP [Mar 2024](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480/9 "Post date") Genius actually, as I was working on this myself. probably end up using this compared to my Franken Python of a mess. IвҖҷll have to see maybe if I could incorporate my real time dictation into something like this, IвҖҷll post it if I end up having something work. [![](https://forum.obsidian.md/letter_avatar_proxy/v4/letter/s/ebca7d/48.png)](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/SudoRmRfMe) [SudoRmRfMe](https://forum.obsidian.md/u/SudoRmRfMe)VIP ![](https://forum.obsidian.md/letter_avatar_proxy/v4/letter/l/3d9bf3/24.png)lymeswold [Mar 2024](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/speech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service/58480/10 "Post date") Do you have GPT plus? Observer](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fill:38:38/1*_s7zTo3ZFNicmn4UHRZrUw.jpeg)](https://medium.com/obsidian-observer?source=post_page---post_publication_sidebar-dc0a52745260-7b2843bf8d64---------------------------------------) Welcome to The Obsidian Observer, a hub for all Obsidian enthusiasts. Whether youвҖҷre a beginner or a seasoned pro, our publication delivers in-depth how-to guides, innovative workflows, and captivating opinions to help unlock your note-taking potential. [Follow publication](https://medium.com/m/signin?actionUrl=https%3A%2F%2Fmedium.com%2F_%2Fsubscribe%2Fcollection%2Fobsidian-observer&operation=register&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fmedium.com%2Fobsidian-observer%2Fspeech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service-7b2843bf8d64&collection=Obsidian+Observer&collectionId=dc0a52745260&source=post_page---post_publication_sidebar-dc0a52745260-7b2843bf8d64---------------------post_publication_sidebar------------------) # Speech-to-text in Obsidian using OpenAI Whisper Service [![TfTHacker](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fill:32:32/1*ZV47V0sTbc72zve9dqnllw.jpeg)](https://medium.com/@tfthacker?source=post_page---byline--7b2843bf8d64---------------------------------------) [TfTHacker](https://medium.com/@tfthacker?source=post_page---byline--7b2843bf8d64---------------------------------------) Follow 5 min read В· Apr 22, 2023 118 2 [Listen](https://medium.com/m/signin?actionUrl=https%3A%2F%2Fmedium.com%2Fplans%3Fdimension%3Dpost_audio_button%26postId%3D7b2843bf8d64&operation=register&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fmedium.com%2Fobsidian-observer%2Fspeech-to-text-in-obsidian-using-openai-whisper-service-7b2843bf8d64&source=---header_actions--7b2843bf8d64---------------------post_audio_button------------------) Share The Obsidian community continues to impress. This month there are several new and updated AI plugins. In this article, I want to highlight one of them that stood out to me that has a lot of potential: The [Whisper plugin](https://obsidian.md/plugins?id=whisper) by [Nik Danilov](https://twitter.com/nikdanilov_). Press enter or click to view image in full size ![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:700/1*cGhCPzrZZH_bEBMiblC8GA.jpeg) > **Please note**, [full disclosure](https://medium.com/@tfthacker/writers-please-write-from-the-heart-not-from-ai-684960e0b44c), I used AI tools to assist in the writing of this article. (Grammarly, gpt-4, and Whisper). # What is Whisper? Whisper is a service provided by [OpenAI](https://openai.com/). Whisper is an Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) system, which means it can convert spoken language into written text. In simpler terms, itвҖҷs a technology that вҖңlistensвҖқ to human speech and transcribes it into text. Whisper is designed to understand and process a wide variety of languages and accents, making it useful for numerous applications. Some common use cases for ASR systems like Whisper include transcription services, voice assistants, real-time captions, and more. To develop Whisper, OpenAI trained the system on a vast amount of multilingual and multitask data collected from the web. This extensive training allows the system to provide accurate transcriptions for everyday computer users, even if they have different accents or speak various languages. # Why do I like Whisper? **First**, it does a _good job and handles multiple languages_. I frequently work in two languages and the ability to do speech-to-text without having to think about it is wonderful. **Second**, it is a _cost-effective_ speech-to-text service. You can request an API key from openAI and you control how much you spend. The API cost is $0.006 a minute. So ten minutes of dictation is 6 pennies. # Using Whisper in Obsidian ## Installation The Whisper plugin can be installed via the Community Plugins tab in Settings in Obsidian. ## API Key Once installed you need to provide it with some information for it to work. As mentioned, Whisper is built on top of the OpenAI Whisper service. To use it, you need an OpenAI API key. If you donвҖҷt have one, you can read this article to get instructions on how to acquire your own key: вҖң [_How to get your own API Key for using OpenAI chatGPT in Obsidian_](https://tfthacker.medium.com/how-to-get-your-own-api-key-for-using-openai-chatgpt-in-obsidian-41b7dd71f8d3)вҖқ Once you obtain your API key from OpenAI, paste it into the API Key field in the Settings tab for the Whisper plugin as illustrated in the following image: Press enter or click to view image in full size ![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:700/1*NWzTOaTdglNrOtF6qapKJA.png) Provide the Whisper plugin with your OpenAI API Key ## Folder for Output by the Plugin The second thing to configure is the template file location. It is called template location, which is a little confusing since it's not a template folder. This is the folder where speech-to-text documents will be stored. I left the folder blank which will put any documents created by the plugin into the root folder of the Obsidian vault. # Using the Plugin The plugin is very simple in what it does. You dictate to it and it creates a markdown file with a transcription. Take a look at the following animated image: Press enter or click to view image in full size ![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:700/1*rmkBXt2TkWu42wPG2zpG4A.gif) There is no audio recorded here, but I was using the plugin. - First, you click on the button on the ribbon bar. It is a little waveform-looking button. - Then you are prompted with a modal for recording. - Click on record and start talking. - When you finish, click the stop button At this point, the plugin will send your audio recording to the Whisper API. - Then the Whisper API will convert your speech into text and return it to the plugin. - The plugin then creates a new markdown file and inserts the transcribed speech. Since this is now saved to a markdown file, you can do all your normal editing in Obsidian as you would do with any other file. # Add this Plugin to our Workflow I will use this plugin in two ways: ## Rapid Capturing of Thought This plugin should encourage us to use speech-to-text to rapidly capture our thoughts. Sometimes I am just too lazy to type, so clicking a button and capturing a thought while it's fresh on the mind and not having to fiddle with the keyboard or editor is a way of reducing mental friction and not losing the idea. ## Long-form Thinking Out loud While the goal of note-taking is to capture our thoughts, there are some subtle differences in how we write when using a keyboard and how we think when expressing ourselves verbally. Each form of capturing thoughts has its advantages. I find speaking very freeing, but also sloppy, allowing for more random thoughts to emerge along with lots of mistakes. It is a bit like speaking with yourself out loud and it gets messy. But at the same time, hearing your thoughts audibly triggers our minds in unique ways. So while taking notes, sometimes I just hit the record button and start talking to Obsidian and allowing the ideas to flow, not being bogged down by the rigor of typing in words, sentences, punctuation, and all the rest of the rigor of writing. Put simply, speaking without restraint can be creative and still capture those words into text that can then be edited later. # The Future of This Plugin This plugin was just released and it needs polishing. Here is what I would like to see: - Ability to transcribe into an existing document at the current cursor location. - Moving the Recording modal into a side pane. In the pane have the recording controls, while still allowing us to freely move around the Obsidian UI as we continue to dictate our thoughts. Our words could even be transcribed into the pane with the ability to easily copy them into my notes later. The idea is to let me reference other notes and Obsidian features while dictating my ideas Having said all this, this plugin is off to a promising start and I look forward to seeing what its developer does with it. Even more so, I am happy to have it as a part of my Obsidian workflow. Thank you for reading this article. рҹҺҷпёҸ [Permalink: Scribe for Obsidian рҹҺҷпёҸ](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe#scribe-for-obsidian-%EF%B8%8F) Transform your voice into insights with Scribe, an Obsidian plugin that not only records your voice and transcribes it, but summarizes, and enriches the note with the power of AI. Dive into a seamless experience where your spoken words are effortlessly converted into a structured, easy-to-navigate knowledge base. Forgot a phrase or concept while recording? Ask "Hey Scribe" followed by a question in the middle of recording and it will fill in the blanks for you ## Screenshots [Permalink: Screenshots](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe#screenshots) [![obsidian-scribe-screenshots](https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/5902658/398481914-79eb4427-799a-47ba-8024-4d1350ac47cf.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..AnJ-dOI98JamYVj8fbza2OoXcP9k5v0DT7PbRmwsiCU)](https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/5902658/398481914-79eb4427-799a-47ba-8024-4d1350ac47cf.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..AnJ-dOI98JamYVj8fbza2OoXcP9k5v0DT7PbRmwsiCU) ## рҹҢҹ Key Features [Permalink: рҹҢҹ Key Features](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe#-key-features) - **Voice-to-Text Magic:** Begin recording and watch as your voice notes are transcribed, summarized, and turned into actionable insights. - **Robust on Failure:** Designed with mobile users in mind, Scribe ensures that no step in the process is a single point of failure. Record, transcribe, and summarize on the go, with each step saved progressively. (WIP) - **Seamless Integration:** Utilizes AssemblyAI or OpenAI Whisper for top-tier transcription accuracy and OpenAI for cutting-edge summarization - **Create your custom templates:** Harness the language models and insert your own custom prompts as template! - **Multi Language Support:** Select your language and go wild! - **Interactive Queries:** Ask questions mid-recording, and Scribe fetches the answers, integrating them directly into your notes. - **Mermaid Chart Creation:** Visualize your thoughts and summaries with automatically generated Mermaid charts, providing a unique perspective on your notes. ## рҹ•№пёҸ Commands [Permalink: рҹ•№пёҸ Commands](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe#%EF%B8%8F-commands) ### From the Ribbon button [Permalink: From the Ribbon button](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe#from-the-ribbon-button) - Either Click Start Recording or Open the Controls Modal ### From the Command Pallette type "Scribe" [Permalink: From the Command Pallette type "Scribe"](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe#from-the-command-pallette-type-scribe) - **Begin Recording with Scribe:** \- Opens the controls modal for you to begin recording - **Transcribe & Summarize Current File:** \- Run this on an open audio file - it will Scribe this file. Very useful for recording offline and later Scribing it - **Fix Mermaid Chart:** \- Sometimes the generated Mermaid Chart is invalid, this will attempt to fix it. ## вҡҷпёҸ Settings / Config [Permalink: вҡҷпёҸ Settings / Config](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe#%EF%B8%8F-settings--config) - **OpenAI API Key (Required):** Essential for transcription and summarization. Set your key in the `Settings`. Get your key here - [Open AI Developer Console - https://platform.openai.com/settings](https://platform.openai.com/settings) - **AssemblyAI Key (Optional):** For enhanced transcription accuracy and optionality of services. Enjoy a $50 credit from AssemblyAI to get started. Get your key in the [AssemblyAI Dev Console https://www.assemblyai.com/app/account](https://www.assemblyai.com/app/account) - **Audio Input Device:** Select which microphone to use for recording. By default, the system's default audio input device will be used. - **Audio File Format:** Choose between WebM and MP3 formats for saving audio recordings. MP3 format will be converted from WebM on the client side. - **Disable LLM Transcription:** If enabled, audio will not be sent to any LLM for transcription, providing privacy when needed. ## рҹҡҖ Getting Started [Permalink: рҹҡҖ Getting Started](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe#-getting-started) ### Installation [Permalink: Installation](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe#installation) 1. In Obsidian, navigate to `Settings` \> `Community Plugins`. 2. Search for `Scribe` and click `Install`. 3. Once installed, toggle `Enable` to activate Scribe. ## рҹ“– How to Use [Permalink: рҹ“– How to Use](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe#-how-to-use) 1. **Start Recording:** Trigger the Scribe action or select it from the ribbon and begin recording 2. **Interactive Queries:** Pose questions during recording to have them answered and integrated into your notes just say "Hey Scribe" followed by the question. 3. **Review and Explore:** Access the transcribed text, summary, insights, and Mermaid charts directly in your note. ## рҹ“ұ Mobile [Permalink: рҹ“ұ Mobile](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe#-mobile) Scribe shines in mobile scenarios, gracefully handling interruptions or connectivity issues. If any step fails, simply resume without losing any progress. This is a WIP, you will never lose your audio, but it will regenerate the note, transcription and summary ### Known Issues [Permalink: Known Issues](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe#known-issues) 1. On iOS, the screen must be **ON** while recording otherwise it won't capture youre voice. This is a limitation of Obsidian. ## рҹӣ  How to Contribute [Permalink: рҹӣ  How to Contribute](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe#-how-to-contribute) Your insights, improvements, and feedback are what make Scribe better. Feel free to submit issues, pull requests, or suggestions to enhance the plugin further. ## рҹҷҸ Acknowledgments [Permalink: рҹҷҸ Acknowledgments](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe#-acknowledgments) An deep bow, acknowledgement and gratitude to the innumerable nameless Humans from Colombia to the Phillipines to Kenya and beyond who used their intelligence and human hearts to help train what we are calling artificial intelligence. [https://www.noemamag.com/the-exploited-labor-behind-artificial-intelligence/](https://www.noemamag.com/the-exploited-labor-behind-artificial-intelligence/) [https://www.wired.com/story/millions-of-workers-are-training-ai-models-for-pennies/](https://www.wired.com/story/millions-of-workers-are-training-ai-models-for-pennies/) A special thanks to [Drew Mcdonald of the Magic Mic Plugin](https://github.com/drewmcdonald/obsidian-magic-mic), this was super useful for learning how to access & use the audio buffers Also a special thanks to [Mossy1022 of the Smart Memos Plugin](https://github.com/Mossy1022/Smart-Memos) including Mermaid Charts is SO useful, and I got that idea from your plugin. ## рҹ”’ License [Permalink: рҹ”’ License](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe#-license) Scribe is released under the MIT License. Feel free to use, modify, and distribute it as you see fit. ## рҹ“¬ Contact [Permalink: рҹ“¬ Contact](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe#-contact) Got questions, feedback, or ideas? Reach out through [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe#) or join our Discord channel to become part of the Scribe community. ## вқ“ FAQ [Permalink: вқ“ FAQ](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe#-faq) **Q: Do I need both OpenAI and AssemblyAI keys?** A: An OpenAI key is essential, while the AssemblyAI key is optional but recommended for better transcription accuracy. **Q: Can I use Scribe offline?** A: Scribe requires an internet connection for transcription and summarization services. You can record offline and later use the Transcribe & Summarize Current File command on the Audio file to Scribe it. * * * Dive into a new era of note-taking with Scribe вҖ“ Where your voice breathes life into ideas. рҹҢҲвңЁ ## About Record, transcribe, and transform voice notes into structured insights. Leverage Whisper or AssemblyAI and ChatGPT to fill in gaps, generate summaries, and visualize ideas вҖ” all seamlessly integrated within Obsidian. ### Topics [openai](https://github.com/topics/openai "Topic: openai") [obsidian](https://github.com/topics/obsidian "Topic: obsidian") [voice-assistant](https://github.com/topics/voice-assistant "Topic: voice-assistant") [assemblyai](https://github.com/topics/assemblyai "Topic: assemblyai") ### Resources [Readme](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe#readme-ov-file) ### License [MIT license](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe#MIT-1-ov-file) [Activity](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/activity) ### Stars [**41**\\ stars](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/stargazers) ### Watchers [**2**\\ watching](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/watchers) ### Forks [**11**\\ forks](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/forks) [Report repository](https://github.com/contact/report-content?content_url=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2FMikodin%2Fobsidian-scribe&report=Mikodin+%28user%29) ## [Releases\ 28](https://github.com/Mikodin/obsidian-scribe/releases) [Scribe 2.1.1 - ## Complete Audio Recording Solution for macOS/Obsidian

Based on my research, here's a comprehensive plan for implementing system audio/meeting audio recording on macOS with Obsidian integration:

### 1. **System Audio Capture Setup (BlackHole + Multi-Output Device)**
   - Install BlackHole (free, open-source virtual audio driver)
   - Configure Audio MIDI Setup:
     - Create Multi-Output Device (for hearing while recording)
     - Create Aggregate Device (for mixing mic + system audio)
   - This enables capturing all system audio including meetings

### 2. **Obsidian Plugin Options**

   **For Basic Recording:**
   - **Super Duper Audio Recorder** - Enhanced core plugin with audio source selection
   - **Better Audio Recorder** - Adds pause/resume functionality
   
   **For AI-Enhanced Recording:**
   - **Obsidian Scribe** (Recommended) - Full-featured with:
     - Whisper/AssemblyAI transcription
     - AI summarization via ChatGPT
     - Custom templates with prompts
     - Mermaid chart generation
     - "Hey Scribe" mid-recording queries
     - Mobile-friendly with offline recording
   
   - **Whisper Plugin** - Simple transcription with OpenAI Whisper
     - Multi-language support
     - Cost-effective ($0.006/minute)
     - Direct API integration

### 3. **Alternative Professional Tools**
   - **Audio Hijack** ($77) - Professional recording with routing
   - **Loopback** ($119) - Advanced audio routing
   - **OBS Studio** (Free) - Screen + audio recording

### 4. **Workflow Implementation**
   1. Set up BlackHole for system audio routing
   2. Install chosen Obsidian plugin(s)
   3. Configure API keys (OpenAI/AssemblyAI)
   4. Create recording templates
   5. Test with sample meeting recording

### 5. **Key Considerations**
   - iOS limitation: Screen must stay on during recording
   - API costs: ~$0.36/hour for Whisper transcription
   - Privacy: Option to disable LLM transcription when needed
   - File formats: WebM (default) or MP3 (converted)

Would you like me to proceed with setting up any of these solutions?

input.plan:
## Simple Audio Recording Setup for macOS/Obsidian (No AI)

Here's a streamlined plan for setting up basic audio recording that captures both system audio and microphone without AI transcription:

### 1. **Install BlackHole (Free Virtual Audio Driver)**
   - Download from: https://existential.audio/blackhole/
   - Install BlackHole 2ch version
   - Allow in System Settings > Privacy & Security

### 2. **Configure Audio MIDI Setup**
   - Open `/Applications/Utilities/Audio MIDI Setup`
   - **Create Multi-Output Device:**
     1. Click + вҶ’ "Create Multi-Output Device"
     2. Check "Built-in Output" FIRST
     3. Then check "BlackHole 2ch"
     4. Check "Drift Correction" for BlackHole
     5. Rename to "Built-in + BlackHole"
   
   - **Create Aggregate Device (for recording):**
     1. Click + вҶ’ "Create Aggregate Device"
     2. Check "BlackHole 2ch" FIRST
     3. Then check your microphone
     4. Check "Drift Correction" for microphone
     5. Rename to "BlackHole Input"

### 3. **Set System Audio Preferences**
   - System Settings > Sound:
     - Output: Select "Built-in + BlackHole"
     - Input: Select "BlackHole Input"

### 4. **Install Obsidian Plugin**
   - **Super Duper Audio Recorder** (Recommended for non-AI use)
     - Features: Audio source selection, pause/resume, folder selection
     - Install via Community Plugins
     - Configure audio input to use "BlackHole Input"
     - Set output folder for recordings
     - Choose format (WebM or MP3)

### 5. **Test Recording**
   - Play some audio (YouTube, meeting, etc.)
   - Start recording in Obsidian
   - Verify both system audio and mic are captured
   - Check audio file is saved correctly

### Benefits:
- Captures ALL system audio (meetings, videos, etc.)
- Records microphone simultaneously
- No API keys or costs required
- Simple pause/resume functionality
- Works offline
- Privacy-focused (no data sent to external services)

Ready to proceed with this setup?

## Updated Audio Recording Setup for macOS/Obsidian (No Built-in Output)

Based on your available audio devices, here's the corrected setup plan:

### 1. **Install BlackHole (if not already installed)**
   - I can see you already have "BlackHole 2ch" in your device list вң“
   - If you need to reinstall: https://existential.audio/blackhole/

### 2. **Configure Multi-Output Device (for hearing while recording)**
   In Audio MIDI Setup (which you have open):
   - Click the "Multi-Output Device" you're currently editing
   - **Check these boxes in this order:**
     1. вң“ Dell WL5024 Headset (already checked - your primary output)
     2. вң“ BlackHole 2ch (check this second)
   - **Enable Drift Correction** for BlackHole 2ch (check the box in the right column)
   - This allows you to hear audio through your headset while also routing it to BlackHole for recording

### 3. **Create Aggregate Device (for recording mic + system audio)**
   - Click the + button вҶ’ "Create Aggregate Device"
   - **Check these devices:**
     1. вң“ BlackHole 2ch (for system audio)
     2. вң“ MacBook Pro Microphone (or Dell WL5024 Headset if you prefer to use the headset mic)
   - Enable "Drift Correction" for the microphone
   - Rename to "Recording Input" or similar

### 4. **Set System Audio Preferences**
   - System Settings > Sound:
     - **Output:** Select "Multi-Output Device"
     - **Input:** Select your new "Recording Input" (Aggregate Device)

### 5. **Install Obsidian Plugin**
   - **Super Duper Audio Recorder**
     - Install via Community Plugins in Obsidian
     - In plugin settings:
       - Set audio input to "Recording Input" (your Aggregate Device)
       - Choose output folder for recordings
       - Select format (WebM or MP3)

### 6. **Test Setup**
   - Play some audio to test system sound capture
   - Start recording in Obsidian
   - Speak to test microphone capture
   - Stop and verify both audio sources are in the recording

### Alternative Devices You Could Use:
- **MacBook Pro Speakers** instead of Dell headset for output
- **Dell WL5024 Headset** microphone if you prefer over MacBook Pro mic
- **External Headphones** if you want to use those

The key is having BlackHole 2ch in your Multi-Output Device to capture system audio while still being able to hear it through your chosen output device.
